_comment.1: SOUNDCARD L->y DELAY by in-loop MULTITONE phase slope. Five well-
_comment.2: separated tones (dodging the known disturbance lines) are summed and
_comment.3: played simultaneously on L, so EACH tone integrates over the whole ~25 s
_comment.4: record -- the max-SNR trick the two-tone proved (single-freq coherent
_comment.5: detection beat the beam drift 8-24x, while white noise and a time-shared
_comment.6: bode sweep did not, coherence ~5%). command (after inject) and error
_comment.7: (before) are recorded per frame on the same clock; DFT both at each tone,
_comment.8: phase(H)=phase_err-phase_cmd, then slope of unwrapped phase vs frequency
_comment.10: so the actuator adds ~0 delay; piplate parked holds the DC bias.

pipeline._note: MULTITONE stimulus on element 0 (->L). amplitude 0.85 with 5 tones = 0.17/tone (peak 0.85, no clip), comparable to the two-tone 0.25/tone. fs 3788 = nominal loop rate, only used to convert Hz->rad/frame; the delay itself comes out in frames (fs-independent).

soundcard actuator, threaded; scale [1,0] drives L only. latency_frames 32 = one full period (0.667 ms queue) -- latency_frames 4 UNDER-DROVE 24x (underran the 32-frame period floor; xrun detection is disabled so it was silent). This queue latency is a KNOWN constant (plugin logs 'queue N fr = X ms') to subtract from the measured delay.
piplate parked, holds the 2 V bias so the beam stays on-sensor
~95000 frames (~25 s at ~3788 Hz) then stop
